content.problemStatement:
JJ had an array AAA of length NNN such that 0≤Ai<2300 \le A_i \lt 2^{30}0≤Ai​<230 for all 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N.

He listed down the XOR of all the subsequences of AAA of size ≥(N−1)\ge (N - 1)≥(N−1) in some **random** order. Let's call the XOR of these (N+1)(N + 1)(N+1) subsequences as B1,B2,…,BN+1B_1, B_2, \ldots, B_{N+1}B1​,B2​,…,BN+1​.

Unfortunately, he lost the initial array AAA. Can you help him recover it?

If there are multiple arrays which satisfy the given conditions, print any. Also, it is guaranteed that corresponding to the given input, an answer always exists.


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the initial array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains (N+1)(N + 1)(N+1) integers B1,B2,…,BN+1B_1, B_2, \ldots, B_{N+1}B1​,B2​,…,BN+1​ - the XOR of all the subsequences of AAA of size ≥(N−1)\ge (N - 1)≥(N−1) in some **random** order.


Output Format
For each test case, output NNN integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ which satisfy the given conditions.


If multiple answers exist, print any.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Note: The above test case belongs to subtask 2.
Let ⊕\oplus⊕ denote the bitwise XOR operation.


Test Case 1: A=[1,1]A = [1, 1]A=[1,1] satisfies all the conditions since [1,0,1]=[1,1⊕1,1][1, 0, 1] = [1, 1 \oplus 1, 1][1,0,1]=[1,1⊕1,1]


Test Case 2: A=[5,4,2,2]A = [5, 4, 2, 2]A=[5,4,2,2] satisfies all the conditions since [5,3,1,4,3]=[5⊕2⊕2,5⊕4⊕2,5⊕4⊕2⊕2,4⊕2⊕2,5⊕4⊕2][5, 3, 1, 4, 3] = [5 \oplus 2 \oplus 2, 5 \oplus 4 \oplus 2, 5 \oplus 4 \oplus 2 \oplus 2, 4 \oplus 2 \oplus 2, 5 \oplus 4 \oplus 2][5,3,1,4,3]=[5⊕2⊕2,5⊕4⊕2,5⊕4⊕2⊕2,4⊕2⊕2,5⊕4⊕2]
